description.from:
## Summary
Make score optimization repeatable with deterministic reference datasets, explicit curation, and evaluation-selection rules.

## Scope
- Deterministic reference datasets are frozen `DataSet` artifacts.
- Reference datasets are single-score datasets.
- Active reference assignment lives on `Score`, not on `DataSet`.
- Scorecard-level examples are the maintained curated source pool unless later work changes that contract.
- Evaluation becomes source-agnostic once the dataset exists.

## Key assumptions
- The benchmark is always the frozen dataset, never live upstream source objects.
- Labels are snapshotted into the dataset artifact at build time.
- Schema-budget cleanup may be required before the preferred curation model can ship.

## Notes
Create the child tasks in this epic exactly as planned so this work stays separate from RCA/reliability fixes.
description.to:
## Summary
Make score optimization repeatable with deterministic associated datasets, explicit curation, and evaluation-selection rules.

## Scope
- Associated datasets are DataSet artifacts linked to a score via DataSet.scoreId.
- Selection is association-only with deterministic newest-first ordering.
- Curation builds deterministic, single-score datasets from vetted labels.
- Evaluation becomes source-agnostic once the dataset exists.

## Key assumptions
- The benchmark is always the associated DataSet artifact, never live upstream source objects.
- Labels are snapshotted into the dataset artifact at build time.
- Schema-budget cleanup may be required before the preferred curation model can ship.

## Notes
Keep this epic separate from RCA reliability and misclassification triage workstreams.
